title: HyDE: Precise Zero-Shot Dense Retrieval without Relevance Labels

defines.name: HyDE
defines.definition: A zero-shot dense retrieval method that generates a hypothetical document from a query with an instruction-following language model and then embeds it with a contrastive encoder for retrieval.

problem: Fully zero-shot dense retrieval is difficult because effective retrieval usually depends on relevance labels or task-specific supervision.
